response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with nearly two decades of software experience, solid TypeScript/Node.js depth, and recent hands-on exposure to MCP servers and agentic-workflow tooling at re:cinq. However, the role requires 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions, and the resume shows roughly one year of GenAI-adjacent work with no demonstrated RAG, vector database, evaluation-pipeline, or multi-agent framework experience, and minimal Python. The gap on the core required qualifications is too large for a Staff Forward Deployed GenAI role, despite otherwise excellent engineering credentials.
strengths: Recent hands-on MCP and AI-agent infrastructure work, directly relevant to agentic workflows
Deep full-stack and systems experience (TypeScript, Node.js, low-latency services, AWS CDK) across 15+ years
Proven mentorship and technical-communication track record via workshops, courses, and conference talks
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8+ years building AI-driven solutions (~1 year of GenAI work)
No evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation, or multi-agent frameworks like LangGraph/CrewAI
Limited Python exposure and no demonstrated customer-facing technical discovery or forward-deployed consulting experience

key_factors.factor: MCP and agentic-tooling experience at current role
Strong overall engineering seniority and cloud/TypeScript foundation
